chunk_summary: Complementary feeding should begin at 6 months alongside breastfeeding, focusing on energy-dense, diverse, and iron-rich foods while ensuring food safety and responsive feeding practices.
learning_objectives: Understand the appropriate timing and readiness indicators for introducing complementary feeding at 6 months.
Learn the importance of dietary diversity, iron-rich foods, and vitamin D supplementation during complementary feeding.
Recognize responsive feeding techniques and strategies for managing feeding during illness.
Identify food safety measures to prevent infections and ensure optimal nutrition.
key_facts: Complementary feeding should start at completed 6 months while continuing breastfeeding on demand.
Initial feeding frequency: 2–3 times per day at 6–8 months, progressing to 3–4 meals plus snacks as the child grows.
Iron-rich foods are essential as breast milk alone cannot meet iron needs beyond early infancy.
Food safety practices like handwashing, clean utensils, and safe storage reduce diarrhea risk.
Breastfeeding should continue up to 2 years or longer while complementary foods become the main energy source.
reference_quote: Complementary feeding should begin at completed 6 months while continuing breastfeeding on demand. (WHO guideline, 2023)
document_key_topics: Complementary feeding initiation and readiness indicators
Dietary diversity and iron-rich foods for infants
Food safety and responsive feeding practices
